summary: Gruber revisits his earlier skepticism about AI-generated voice cloning, acknowledging that the technology has rapidly advanced to the point where synthetic voices are now freakishly convincing. He highlights a demo where someone trained a clone of Steve Jobs's voice and connected it to ChatGPT, producing results that sound remarkably like Jobs. The post pivots from technological marvel to existential concern, warning that vocal deepfakes will inevitably be weaponized in political campaigns to create fake scandals. Gruber argues that the general public and news media are unprepared for a world where real recordings can be dismissed as fake and fabricated ones passed off as genuine.
key_points: AI voice cloning has advanced dramatically in just months, moving from stilted imitations to freakishly uncanny reproductions
The technology will inevitably be weaponized by political operatives to fabricate scandalous audio recordings
The epistemological problem is two-sided: real recordings will be dismissed as fake while fake recordings will be presented as real
The general public and news media organizations are unprepared for the era of convincing vocal deepfakes
Conspiracy theories like Pizzagate gained traction without deepfakes — compelling forgeries will make disinformation far more potent
money_quotes: It's all fun and games in these demos, but this is inevitably going to be put to use by ratfuckers to create fake scandals in political campaigns.
Real recordings will be called fake and fake recordings will be leaked as purportedly real.
I don't think the general population is prepared for this, and I worry that news media organizations aren't either.
A dangerous chunk of wingnuts bought into such a conspiracy in 2016 without compelling deepfake forgeries.
The only hitch is that it sounds like Jobs reading from a script, not speaking extemporaneously. But damned if it doesn't sound like him.
key_insight: The rapid maturation of vocal deepfake technology creates an asymmetric threat to democracy: fabricated audio will be used to smear, while the mere existence of the technology gives cover to dismiss authentic recordings as fake.
